examples:
Command Example
!tn-list-packages limit=1
Context Example
{
"TaniumPackage": [
{
"Command": "/bin/bash run-add-intel-package.sh 2>&1",
"CommandTimeout": 600,
name: tn-list-packages

outputs.description: The command to run. TaniumPackage.CommandTimeout Timeout in seconds for the command execution.
outputs.name: TaniumPackage.Command

outputs.description: The ID of the content set to associate with the package. TaniumPackage.ContentSet.Name The name of the content set to associate with the package.
outputs.name: TaniumPackage.ContentSet.Id

outputs.description: The time and date when this object was created in the database. TaniumPackage.DisplayName The name of the package that displays in the user interface.
outputs.name: TaniumPackage.CreationTime

outputs.description: Timeout in seconds for the action expiry. TaniumPackage.ID The unique ID of the package_spec object.
outputs.name: TaniumPackage.ExpireSeconds

outputs.description: The user who most recently modified this object. TaniumPackage.LastUpdate The most recent time and date when this object was modified.
outputs.name: TaniumPackage.LastModifiedBy

outputs.description: The domain of the user who most recently modified this object. TaniumPackage.ModUser.Id The ID of the user who most recently modified this object.
outputs.name: TaniumPackage.ModUser.Domain

outputs.description: The name of the user who most recently modified this object. TaniumPackage.ModificationTime The most recent time and date when this object was modified.
outputs.name: TaniumPackage.ModUser.Name

outputs.description: The unique name of the package_spec object. TaniumPackage.SourceId The ID of the package into which the parameters are substituted.
outputs.name: TaniumPackage.Name

outputs.description: A verification failure timeout. The time begins with the start of the action. If the action cannot be verified by the timeout, the action status is reported as failed.
outputs.name: TaniumPackage.VerifyExpireSeconds
